plan_binding_snapshot.title: CP00-566 RP18.P05.M04.S13-RP18.P05.M06.S08 generated_from_weighted_implementation_ledger
plan_binding_snapshot.renumbering_policy: Pack IDs are monotonic after the latest live closeout pack. If a requested locked queue skips live-open ledger units, live-open units are inserted first and requested packs are shifted with correction_reason.

implementation_summary.summary: CP00-566 implements descriptor-only coverage of the RP18.P05 workflow permission slice: the M04 Secondary Workflow Slice tail (the ten remaining rows), the full M05 Permission And Audit Binding cycle (twenty-two rows), and the M06 Synthetic Fixture Set head (the first eight rows) as three descriptor-only sections over 40 planned rows with Hermes evidence (H18), Claude review packet (C18), and CP00-567 handoff, without opening AI legal workflows, precedent, clause markup, or DD extraction runtime or any other closed runtime surface.

parsed_review.summary: CP00-566 is a clean, descriptor-only closeout pack (RP18 AI Legal Workflows, P05 workflow permission slice) covering 40 units (RP18.P05.M04.S13–RP18.P05.M06.S08, M04 tail 10 / M05 full 22 / M06 head 8). Read-only inspection via Read/Grep/Glob found the pack internally consistent and independently corroborated across registry.js, service.js, validators.js, the contract JSON, the manifest, the test suite, and the live closeout-pack-plan ledger (source_ledger_sha 13c7ed65…). Deliverable distribution (implementation 14 / fixture 14 / test 6 / hermes_evidence 2 / claude_review 2 / security_audit 2) and micro-title distribution match the prompt baseline, the manifest delivery_counts, and CP566_REQUIREMENTS exactly; required section rows match the live plan's included_units, so coverage validation is not circular against the manifest snapshot. No-write/no-real-data boundaries are structurally enforced: the source contains no fs/network/child_process/database/eval paths (only frozen descriptor objects), and the NO_WRITE_ATTESTATION spread chain CP551→CP566 preserves writes_product_state=false, dispatches_*_runtime=false, emits_hermes_runtime_receipt=false, promotes_claude_to_final_approval=false, and claims_enterprise_trust_from_local_validation=false. The authority boundary is honored: claude_is_final_approval=false, local_validation_claims_enterprise_trust=false, human_final_approval_required_for_runtime_opening=true; the Claude packet is read_only with tools [Read,Grep,Glob]; Hermes patterns are used only for deterministic descriptor evidence/receipt shape, not product runtime. The pack does not overclaim: manifest and command-evidence report production_ready=false, runtime_ready=false, implementation_layer=descriptor, status=implemented_pending_review, and the descriptor-verification production_ready_flag is supported by passing coverage/slice validation, dedicated CP566 tests, the contract-to-code deepEqual pin in the validator script, and all listed validation commands at exit 0. Readiness auth-preflight passed with valid non-empty JSON. No P0/P1/P2/P3 findings; pack is suitable to proceed to deterministic closeout and human final approval.

commit_policy.message: Close CP00-566 ai legal workflows core p05 workflow permission slice descriptor pack
